refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall analyze the worksite area to identify terrain features, obstacles, and work-zone boundaries to generate a comprehensive worksite model/map.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining it clarifies the specific analysis tasks (terrain, obstacles, work-zone) that collectively fulfill the worksite mapping requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by comparing generated worksite model/map against ground truth data from site surveys and sensor inputs, ensuring all key features are accurately represented.
sub_frs: FR1: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area.
FR2: Detect and represent obstacles within the worksite.
FR3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and areas.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use multi-sensor data fusion algorithms combining LIDAR, camera, and GPS data for terrain and obstacle analysis.
DP2: Implement a geospatial mapping engine to generate and update the worksite model/map.
DP3: Apply rule-based work-zone delineation based on operational policies and detected site features.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use multi-sensor data fusion algorithms combining LIDAR, camera, and GPS data for terrain and obstacle analysis.

potential_sources: Data dependencies between terrain and obstacle analysis algorithms.
Interface dependencies between mapping engine and sensor data inputs.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups sensor data fusion for terrain and obstacle analysis, minimizing cross-module coupling by isolating mapping and policy application.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and reliability of sensor data (LIDAR, camera, GPS).
Risk of incomplete or noisy data affecting model accuracy.
High safety criticality requires robust fault detection and fallback strategies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to maintain modularity and reduce complexity at this level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized processing and analysis of multi-sensor data to produce the worksite model/map.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data streams (LIDAR, camera, GPS) from vehicles and site sensors.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model/map data to planning and execution modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Issues alerts if detected obstacles or terrain features pose safety risks.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the module centralizes sensor data fusion and mapping; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined.
Limited containment; vehicle mainly provides raw data, not responsible for analysis.
Minimal containment; teleoperation focuses on control, not mapping.
Some involvement in safety alerts but not in core mapping functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite mapping function within the NGHE Solution module optimizes data fusion and model generation, minimizing cross-module dependencies and improving safety-critical integration.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too large or complex.
Risk of delayed updates if data processing is not optimized.
Integration challenges with Safety Services for timely alerting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider splitting mapping and alerting functions in future levels if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by addressing terrain and obstacle analysis through sensor data fusion, with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion risk if SafetyAlert interface requires frequent updates from NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment is strong within NGHE Solution; interfaces are well-defined and limited in number.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing data flows through Telemetry and Tasking interfaces and validating SafetyAlert triggers.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan for future decomposition to separate mapping and safety alerting if complexity grows.
Define performance requirements to avoid processing bottlenecks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a suitable DP, and assigns an appropriate IM with manageable risks and clear interfaces. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area.
FR2: Detect and represent obstacles within the worksite.
FR3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and areas.

revise.why: Current decomposition and assignments meet criteria; future revisions will be based on next-level analysis.

assumptions: Sensor data is reliable and timely.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient processing capacity.
open_risks: Potential integration delays with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of NGHE Solution processing.
Verification test results of worksite model accuracy.
